Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'S Untimely And Improper Supplemental Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Aug-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ZAHIR NASERI's Motion To Strike Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'S Untimely And Improper Supplemental Discovery Responses.

Plaintiff's motion to strike supplemental responses is granted in part and denied in part.

The motion is denied as to special interrogatory 11. Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.310(a) expressly allows the supplement. It provides "[w]ithout leave of court, a party may serve an amended answer to any interrogatory that contains information subsequently discovered, inadvertently omitted, or mistakenly stated in the initial interrogatory." Plaintiff can use the repudiated answer as impeachment. (Id.) Plaintiff is effectively asking the court to hold that the repudiated answer is binding, but this motion does not meet the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.310(b), (c).

The motion is granted as to RFA 12. A party may not unilaterally amend/supplement an RFA. Rather, Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.300(a) requires leave of court to do so and defendant has not obtained such leave. Uber's supplemental response to RFA 12 is stricken from the record.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Request For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ZAHIR NASERI's Motion And Request For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Zahir Naseri's unopposed motion for leave to file an amended complaint is GRANTED.

"The court may, in furtherance of justice, and on any terms as may be proper, allow a party to amend any pleading or proceeding by adding or striking out the name of any party, or by correcting a mistake in the name of a party, or a mistake in any other respect; and may, upon like terms, enlarge the time for answer or demurrer. The court may likewise, in its discretion, after notice to the adverse party, allow, upon any terms as may be just, an amendment to any pleading or proceeding in other particulars; and may upon like terms allow an answer to be made after the time limited by this code." (Code Civil Procedure section 473(a)(1).) "[T]here is a strong policy in favor of liberal allowance of amendments." (Foxborough v. Van Atta (1994) 26 Cal.App.4th 217, 230.) "Great liberality is indulged in matters of amendment to the end that lawsuits may be determined upon their merits." (Desny v. Wilder (1956) 46 Cal.2d 715, 751.) "[I]t is an abuse of discretion to deny leave to amend where the opposing party was not misled or prejudiced by the amendment." (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Superior Court. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) "It is irrelevant that new legal theories are introduced as long as the proposed amendments 'relate to the same general set of facts.'" (Id.)

Plaintiff here seeks to add new legal theories, but his amendments relate to the same general set of facts, which, broadly speaking, encompass his work with/through Uber. Uber's response in lieu of opposition comments on the deficiencies and breadth of the proposed amendment but asserts no grounds for the court to deny leave to amend. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file an amended complaint is granted. Plaintiff must file his amended complaint forthwith, but no later than August 22, 2025. Plaintiff should present this order at the time he presents the amended complaint for filing.

Plaintiffs must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jun-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Zahir Naseri's motion for summary adjudication is denied.

The motion is both procedurally and substantively deficient. The motion does not identify any cause of action, affirmative defense, claim for damages, or issue of duty that is sought to be adjudicated. (CCP 437c(f)(1)). The court's own review of the complaint and defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s first amended answer discloses only one possible cause of action and no affirmative defense, claim for damages or issue of duty that in any way refers to the sole issue of whether Mr. Naseri is an employe or independent contractor raised in the motion. That cause of action is the fifth cause of action which alleges that Uber "violated Labor Code (Misclassification) AB5." However, even assuming that misclassification is a cognizable claim, at a minimum that claim includes the additional elements of causation and harm, neither of which Mr. Naseri addresses in his motion and thus the motion is procedurally deficient per CCP 437c(f)(1). The substantive deficiency is that the motion is based on the argument that the "ABC test" applies to Uber drivers, but that is contrary to California law Per Proposition 22, the applicable test is set forth in Bus. & Prof. Code 7451. (Castellanos v. State of California (2024) 16 Cal. 5th 588).

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)

PLAINTIFF ZAHIR NASERI's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Plaintiff Zahir Naseri's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to portions of defendant Uber Technologies' first amended answer is granted as to the fourteenth affirmative defense purporting to reserve the right to allege additional affirmative defenses and denied as to all other portions of the amended answer for which Mr. Naseri seeks judgment in his favor. Uber's general denial and all other affirmative defenses challenged on this motion, including those based on an asserted arbitration agreement, are adequately alleged. Mr. Naseri's declaration was not considered in ruling on this motion since a motion for judgment on the pleadings is a non-evidentiary motion. Mr. Naseri's request for judicial notice is denied because it seeks judicial notice of matters that are not judicially noticeable.

Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ZAHIR NASERI's MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff Zahir Naseri's motion to strike Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc's answer is OFF CALENDAR. A party moving to strike a pleading or a part of a pleading must meet and confer with the party who filed the pleading "[b]efore filing [the] motion." (CCP section 435.5(a).) The meeting must be "in person, by telephone or by video conference." (Ibid.) The meeting must take place at least five days before the date the motion must be filed. (CCP section 435.5(a)(2.) The moving party must file a declaration with its moving papers demonstrating compliance with these requirements and/or that the opposing party failed to confer in good faith. (CCP section 435.5(3).) The court has carefully reviewed the record to date. Clearly, the meet and confer requirements were not timely met. The motion is, therefore, ordered off calendar. Plaintiff may file a new motion based on the January 2025 meet and confer, but in doing so he must comply with section 425.5's requirements, including the requirement for an appropriate declaration. The parties MUST provide courtesy copies of all filings to the department, consistent with the Local Rules. Failure to do so may result in a continuance or the motion being ordered off calendar.
